PLAINTIFF/APPLICANT KONSTANTINOS DIMOYANIS' APPLICATION FOR WAIVER OF ADDITIONAL COURT FEES AND COSTS WAS HELD. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF/APPLICANT. THE COURT DENIED WAIVER OF ADDITIONAL FEES WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS THE APPLICATION DID NOT OFFER ANY INFORMATION REGARDING FINANCIAL STATUS. ORDER DENYING WAIVER OF FEE SENT TO ALL PARTIES. JUDGE ROBERT L. DONDERO; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: JOANN BRYCE CSR #3321 (206)

LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA STATE PERSONNEL BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO JOIN AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/PM) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO FILE OR SERVE THE PETITION WITHIN THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. NEITHER THE FACTS ON THE FACE OF THE PETITION NOR THE UNPLEADED FACTS IN PETITIONER'S DECLARATION PROVIDE ANY BASIS FOR TOLLING OR EXTENDING THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/PM) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Konstantinos Dimoyannis, 510-428-9004, petitioner and responding party appearing in propria persona
Christopher W. Frick of the Office of the Director, Legal Department, 703-4240, appearing for real party in interest and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST Department of Industrial Relations' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is sustained without leave to amend for failure to file or serve the petition within the one year statute of limitations. Neither the facts on the face of the petition nor the unpleaded facts in petitioner's declaration provide any basis for tolling or extending the limitations period. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Konstantinos Dimoyannis, 510-428-9004, petitioner and responding party appearing in propria persona
Dorothy Egel, 916-653-1403, of the State Personnel Board, appearing via Court Call for moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA STATE PERSONNEL BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is sustained without leave to amend for failure to join an indispensable party. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Dec-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff and Opposing Party Konstantinos Dimoyannis, (510) 428-9004, appeared in Propria Persona
RULING:
No other appearances on this matter. After the court room clerk verified and confirmed that the tentative ruling was recorded accurately on the IVR system and there being no compliance with the tentative ruling rule, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Real Party in Interest California Department of Industrial Relations' demurrer to petition of administrative mandamus is sustained with 10 days leave to amend from notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to submit proposed order. (301/PJB/) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR# 11604

Notice Of Motion Of Joinder Of The California State Personnel Board In Demurrer
Off Calendar Dec-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff and Opposing Party Konstantinos Dimoyannis, (510) 428-9004, appeared in Propria Persona
RULING:
No other appearances on this matter. After the court room clerk verified and confirmed that the tentative ruling was recorded accurately on the IVR system and there being no compliance with the tentative ruling rule, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA STATE PERSONNEL BOARD'S Joinder in Real Party in Interest California Department of Industrial Relations' demurrer to petition of administrative mandamus IS GRANTED. Prevailing party to submit proposed order. (301/PJB/) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR# 11604
